steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage maps a geographic mirror: north China immigrants (Mandarin-speaking) settle in Flushing; south China immigrants (Cantonese/Fuzhounese-speaking) settle in Chinatown. The question asks what best explains why each neighborhood maintains its distinct language — so the correct answer must trace this pattern back to regional origin.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: claims older immigrants are multilingual — no such detail appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — geographic language differences within China are reflected in NYC settlement patterns: directly explains the consistent matching between each group's regional origin and their neighborhood's dominant language. ✓
  • Choice C: suggests immigrants switch neighborhoods over time — the passage describes stable geographic separation, not movement. ✗
  • Choice D: introduces population totals as the explanation — the passage never mentions population size as a factor. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
